subject: Breast
title: The Breast Risk Ladder Decoded
hook: Not every breast lesion carries the same cancer risk.

points.heading: The risk ladder
points.icon: a ladder with breast tissue on each rung
bullets: Nonproliferative change carries no extra cancer risk
Atypia pushes risk up to 4 to 5-fold

points.heading: LCIS vs DCIS
bullets: LCIS is a bilateral risk marker, about 8 to 10-fold
DCIS is true noninvasive carcinoma, already malignant
DCIS needs surgical excision, unlike the lower rungs

points.heading: ALH vs ADH
points.icon: two side by side duct cross-sections
bullets: Atypical lobular hyperplasia parallels atypical ductal hyperplasia
Both show some but not all in situ features
Neither one is the same as true carcinoma

points.heading: Why it matters
bullets: Tamoxifen is used for high-risk benign disease
It blocks oestrogen effects in breast tissue
This is risk reduction, not active cancer treatment

captionLines: Not every breast lesion carries the same cancer risk. 📈
Proliferative changes raise risk 1.5 to 2-fold. Atypia pushes it to 4 to 5-fold. LCIS raises it 8 to 10-fold. DCIS is already cancer rather than a risk marker.
These risk markers guide surveillance and chemoprevention. DCIS itself needs surgical excision rather than watching.
Recall Engine turns any lecture into SBAs, MCQs, flashcards and high-yield notes so risk stratification numbers stop blurring together.
